topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies for promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of solar panels on energy consumption
doc2: case studies on energy efficiency improvements

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy sources and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of wind energy on carbon footprint

topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various innovative approaches and technologies aimed at promoting renewable energy and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of wind energy on carbon footprint
doc3: role of biofuels in sustainable development
comparison of renewable vs. fossil fuels

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Access
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving access to Medicare services and benefits for different populations, including seniors, individuals with disabilities, and those requiring specific medical treatments.

Document 1: Preserve Patient Access to Reputable DMEPOS Providers Act of 2009
exempts from the surety bond requirement any state-licensed retail pharmacy that has not been subject to such an adverse action for at least 10 years.
Document 2: Helping Fill the Medicare Rx Gap Act of 2009
count costs incurred in providing prescription drugs by the Indian Health Service, a federally-qualified health center.
Document 3: Equity in Social Security Act of 2010
authorize the treatment of permanent partnerships between individuals of the same gender as marriage for purposes of determining entitlement to OASDI benefits.
Document 4: Patient-Doctor Freedom Act of 2010
prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services from precluding an enrollee from entering into any contract or arrangement for health care with any health care provider.
Document 5: Comprehensive Immunosuppressive Drug Coverage for Kidney Transplant Patients Act of 2011
extend the months of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs for kidney transplant patients.
Document 6: Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers Act of 2007
prepare and publish a monthly Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers.
Document 7: Tele-Care Act of 2009
provide for coverage under Medicare part B of unscheduled physician telephone services.
Document 8: Home Health Care Access Protection Act of 2010
amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to the prospective payment system for home health services.
Document 9: Geriatric Assessment and Chronic Care Coordination Act of 2007
provide for Medicare part B coverage of geriatric assessments and chronic care coordination services.
Document 10: Ensuring Seniors Access to Local Pharmacies Act of 2015
direct any prescription drug plan that has in its network one or more pharmacies located in a health professional shortage area.

topic.name: Tax Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax credits, deductions, and treatment of specific expenses and contributions.

1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax credit for increasing research activities
2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the classification, for depreciation purposes, of any motorsports entertainment complex as seven-year property.
3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the exclusion from gross income of distributions from individual retirement accounts made for charitable purposes.
exclusion from gross income of distributions for charitable purposes.
4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the Hope Scholarship and Lifetime Learning tax credits with a new American Opportunity Tax Credit
allows an income tax credit of up to $2,500 of the qualified tuition and related expenses.
5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase and make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
expand the qualifying advanced energy project credit.
6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for the production of cellulosic biofuel
Defines 'cellulosic biofuel' as any liquid transportation fuel derived from any lignocellulosic or hemicellulosic matter.
7: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the tax treatment of qualified tuition programs
allows an individual to deduct up to $5,000 of the aggregate contributions.
8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2011 the tax deduction for certain expenses of elementary and secondary school teachers
the tax credit for holders of qualified zone academy bonds.
9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the Indian employment tax credit
accelerated depreciation of business property used on Indian reservations.
10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny employers a tax deduction for payments of excessive compensation
require such employers to file a report on compensation paid to their employees.

topic.name: Trade and Regulatory Amendments
topic.description: The documents primarily focus on amendments related to trade regulations, tariffs, and the control of substances in the U.S.

1: Synthetic Drug Control Act of 2012 - Amends the Controlled Substances Act to add as schedule I controlled substances.
Extends the period for which the Attorney General may temporarily schedule a substance in schedule I to avoid an imminent hazard to public safety.
2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the suspension of duty on Chloroacetic acid, sodium salt.
3: K-1 Visa Program Suspension Act of 2015 prohibits the admission of K-1 nonimmigrants and the issuance of K-1 visas.
4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on urea, polymer with formaldehyde.
5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on modacrylic staple fibers.
6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic fiber tow.
7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on parts and accessories of optical instruments.
8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on waste of camel hair.
9: United States-Brazil Ethanol Open Market Agreements Act - Authorizes the President to enter into negotiations with Brazil.
10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain pressure distillation columns.

topic.name: National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislative measures related to national security, intelligence operations, and military personnel management.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various agencies including the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI), CIA, and DOD.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Defense to transfer members of the Armed Forces receiving outpatient care to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for care.
Directs that the VA-chosen care be provided at the VA facility closest to the permanent duty station or residence of such member.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish within each military department a board of review to review determinations previously made regarding mental conditions not amounting to disability.
Requires the establishment of such Boards and notification of covered individuals regarding their right to a separation review.
Document 5: Requires that amounts made available under the Iraq Security Forces Fund shall be provided as loans subject to full repayment to the U.S. government.
Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2006 to provide conditions for the Commanders' Emergency Response Program.
Document 6: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various agencies including the DNI, CIA, and DOD.
Allows the DNI to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2012 when necessary for important intelligence functions.
Document 7: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on EMP weapons programs.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.
Document 8: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various agencies including the DNI, CIA, and DOD.
Specifies that the amounts authorized for such activities are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of the Air Force to maintain the current leadership rank, aircraft, and core functions of the 354th Fighter Wing at Eielson Air Force Base.
Prohibits changes until completion of an environmental impact statement.
Document 10: Prohibits the Attorney General from initiating action against any foreign person detained by the United States without consulting key intelligence officials.
Requires an annual report from the AG to Congress on such actions.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and recognition of federal lands and resources, including provisions for wildlife conservation, tribal land restoration, and public land use.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Land Transaction Facilitation Act to reauthorize the program for the completion of appraisals and satisfaction of other legal requirements for the sale or exchange of public land
identified for disposal under approved land use plans under the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976.
Document 2: authorizes the Department of the Interior to award grants to wildlife or veterinary institutions
for the conservation and management of the health of endangered, threatened, and sensitive species.
Document 3: Amends the Fish and Wildlife Coordination Act to establish the Wildlife and Hunting Heritage Conservation Council Advisory Committee
to advise the Secretaries of the Interior and of Agriculture on wildlife and habitat conservation.
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana
Directs the Secretary to acquire, for the benefit of the Tribe, trust title to 200 acres of land.
Document 5: Amends the Indian Reorganization Act to apply the Act to all federally recognized Indian tribes
Ratifies and confirms any action taken by the Secretary pursuant to the Act for any Indian tribe.
Document 6: Amends the Clean Air Act to redefine the term 'renewable biomass'
harvested from National Forest System land or public lands in accordance with federal and state law.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal lands
Requires the Connell Lake conveyance area to be managed in accordance with the terms of the Recreation and Public Purposes Act.
Document 9: Declares a parcel of remediated land of the Fort Wingate Depot Activity in New Mexico to be held in trust
for the Zuni Tribe of the Zuni Reservation and part of the Zuni Reservation.
Document 10: Designates specified wilderness areas in New Mexico and administered by the Bureau of Land Management as wilderness
Sets forth requirements regarding land acquisition, livestock grazing, military overflights, and buffer zones.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs and Support Programs
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives aimed at improving education, healthcare, and support services for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Revises provisions concerning Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) use of state approving agencies for approving courses of education for veterans
Establishes performance measures to assess the effectiveness of agencies in services for which they are reimbursed
Document 2: Terminates the FHA Refinancing Program for veterans and directs HUD to study its impact on homeowners who are veterans
Requires the HUD Secretary to publish a statement on the termination of the program
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish an Ombudsman for Recovering Servicemembers to act as a liaison for recovering servicemembers
Defines as 'recovering servicemembers' those in a medical hold or holdover status due to an injury
Document 4: Redefines the term 'period of war' for purposes of eligibility for veterans' pension benefits
Includes any period of active military service for which a veteran received an expeditionary medal
Document 5: Increases the amount authorized to be paid by the VA for improvements and structural alterations for homes of veterans with service-connected disabilities
Specifies different amounts based on the severity of the disability
Document 6: Provides for retroactive calculation of qualifying active duty days for Reserve members to reduce eligibility age for retired pay
Document 7: Directs TSA to develop a process to support and facilitate the movement of severely injured or disabled Armed Forces members through passenger screening
Requires maintaining an operations center for this purpose
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to report on barriers to health care for women veterans
Requires the development of a plan to improve VA health care services to women veterans
Document 9: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to extend protections for servicemembers regarding contracts and mortgages
Prohibits the denial of credit to a servicemember solely by reason of entitlement to protections under SCRA
Document 10: Directs the VA to establish a continuing medical education program for non-VA medical professionals
Aims to increase knowledge of medical conditions common to veterans and improve outreach to veterans and family members

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing various aspects of immigration, including enforcement, fraud prevention, and the treatment of specific groups such as unaccompanied minors and STEM professionals.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to stay the removal of an alien charged with an aggravated felony under state law.
pending disposition of the criminal charge.
Document 2: Addresses the care and custody of unaccompanied alien children.
children under the age of 18 with no lawful immigration status.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to consider, for data sharing purposes only, that when Department of State personnel authorized by the Secretary of State to grant, refuse, revoke, deny, or adjudicate U.S. passports are lawfully engaged in authorized data sharing activities.
those activities shall be considered law enforcement activities involving the administration of criminal justice.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to subject to a fine, up to five years in prison, or both a person who knowingly and falsely represents that he or she is an attorney or accredited representative authorized to represent aliens in immigration proceedings.
direct the Attorney General (DOJ) to compile and update a list of persons who have provided pro bono representation during the most recent 12-month period to aliens in removal proceedings.
Document 5: Provides immigration relief for aliens who are determined by the Secretary of Homeland Security to have obtained nonimmigrant status on or after January 1, 1980.
Extends relief to the spouse and children of eligible aliens granted permanent resident status.
Document 6: This legislation amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require mandatory detention of any alien who is unlawfully present in the United States.
DHS must complete the removal proceedings within 90 days.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to set worldwide employment-based immigration levels.
Makes up to 55,000 (EB-6) visas available in FY2014 and subsequent fiscal years to qualified immigrants who have a doctorate degree in a field of science, technology, engineering, or mathematics.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to submit to Congress a border protection strategy for the international land borders of the United States.
Specifies strategy elements.
Document 9: Revises requirements with respect to the training of aliens and other individuals designated by the Secretary of Homeland Security in the operation of certain aircraft.
Directs the Secretary to require Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) certification of flight schools.
Document 10: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to revise deadline and funding provisions for certain fencing and security project requirements along the southern border.

topic.name: Drug and Medical Device Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the regulation, labeling, and safety of drugs and medical devices in the healthcare system.

Document 1: require the label of a drug containing an opioid to prominently state that the drug contains an opioid and addiction to the drug is possible.
Document 2: promulgate regulations regarding the authorship, content, format, and dissemination requirements for patient medication information (PMI) for prescription drugs.
Document 3: require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to conditionally approve certain cellular therapeutic products without initiation of large-scale clinical trials.
Document 4: Prohibits a manufacturer from distributing a covered product unless the manufacturer holds a certification from the U.S. Customs and Border Protection.
Document 5: require an application for the approval of a medical device or a product development protocol to include: a description of any pediatric subpopulations that suffer from the disease or condition that the device is intended to treat.
Document 6: require an applicant for approval of a new animal drug that is a medically important antimicrobial to demonstrate that there is a reasonable certainty of no harm to human health.
Document 7: Requires covered products to bear labels that state completely and accurately all product ingredients.
Document 8: Gives an individual the right to be treated by a health care practitioner with any medical treatment that the individual desires.
Document 9: deem as misbranded a food or meat food product that contains any part of a cloned animal and does not bear a conspicuous label.
Document 10: authorize an ultimate user or care taker to dispose of a controlled substance in accordance with a prescribed state program.

topic.name: Transportation Security and Safety
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing security and safety measures across various modes of transportation including rail and air travel.

Document 1: vulnerability assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation
grants to Amtrak for fire and life-safety improvements
Document 2: TSA to conduct security screening at small airports
ensure implementation does not affect other airports
Document 3: NTSB's authority to investigate accidents affecting transportation safety
accident investigation training of NTSB employees
Document 4: requires air carriers to refund passenger baggage fees
unfair practice for air carriers regarding baggage fees
Document 5: study the feasibility of technology to improve aircraft communications
pinpoint the location of missing aircraft
Document 6: prohibits compensation for essential air service to communities near major airports
establishes Office of Rural Aviation
Document 7: exemptions from airman certification for micro drones
operated within specific safety parameters
Document 8: appropriates funds for Amtrak tunnel safety projects
rehabilitate tunnels in major cities
Document 9: requires railroad carriers to inform individuals of health risks from hazmat releases
develop safety and security risk assessment tools
Document 10: minimum dimensions for passenger seats on aircraft
regulations for passenger safety and health

topic.name: Employment and Unemployment Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on unemployment benefits, worker protections, and reemployment assistance.

Document 1: Amends the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act
Requires an employer to give 90-day written notice before ordering a plant closing or mass layoff.
Document 2: Amends the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act
Provides that a claim that an individual qualifies for compensation or benefits under such Act shall be considered notwithstanding any denial of any other claim.
Document 3: Establish the Unemployment Tuition Assistance Program
Making tuition assistance available to individuals who became or become unemployed.
Document 4: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008
Requires a further additional Tier-5 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA.
Document 5: Prohibits employers from entering into not to compete covenants with low-wage employees
Requires an employer of such employees to post notice of such prohibition.
Document 6: Provides for federal-state agreements during a period of rising unemployment
Requires federal payments to states to cover 100% of such temporary extended unemployment compensation payments.
Document 7: Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program
Postpones the termination of the program until June 9, 2012.
Document 8: Authorize the Secretary of Labor to enter into agreements with applicant states
To expedite the reemployment of individuals who establish initial eligibility for unemployment compensation.
Document 9: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend requirements.
Document 10: Prohibits employers from entering into not to compete covenants with low-wage employees
Defines 'low-wage employee' as an employee who earns less than $15 per hour.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative measures aimed at addressing environmental regulations, public safety, land use, and support for specific communities and industries.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Project Act of 1939 to authorize small conduit hydropower development.
Requires the Administrators of the Bonneville Power Administration to include in monthly billings estimates and reports of costs related to compliance with federal environmental laws.
Document 2: Directs NASA to encourage women and girls to study science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM).
NASA shall submit to Congress a specified plan on how NASA can best facilitate and support both current and retired astronauts.
Document 3: Authorizes the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to waive any emission standard or other requirements for hazardous air pollutants.
Requires the Administrator to approve or disapprove a request for such a waiver within 90 days.
Document 4: Grants any state sole authority to promulgate or enforce any regulation, guidance, or permit requirement with regard to the underground injection of fluids.
Makes the underground injection of fluids on federal land subject to the law of the state in which that land is located.
Document 5: Authorizes the Attorney General to provide safety training and technical assistance to local law enforcement agencies.
Amends the Homeland Security Act to permit states to use grant funds to improve security at courthouses.
Document 6: Directs the Department of the Interior to take into trust 4 tracts of federal land for the benefit of 19 specified pueblos.
Prohibits gaming on the federal lands taken into trust under this Act.
Document 7: Repeals a statutory restriction on using a medical treatment facility of the Department of Defense to perform an abortion.
Document 8: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs temporary lodging facility in Indianapolis as the 'Dr. Otis Bowen Veteran House.'
Document 9: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1900 West Gray Street as the 'Hazel Hainsworth Young Post Office Building.'
Document 10: Directs the Department of the Interior to conduct a special resource study of the Camp Nelson Civil War Heritage Park.
Evaluate the site's national significance and determine the suitability for designation as a unit of the National Park System.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing financial assistance, resources, and opportunities for small businesses, including those owned by veterans and in low-income areas.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide financial assistance for crop disaster assistance
Directs the Secretary to make grants to state agriculture departments for direct economic loss payments
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to include the acquisition of goods and services through federal supply schedule for veteran-owned small businesses
Document 3: Requires the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to participate in the new markets venture capital program
Increases amounts allocated for investment in small businesses located in low-income geographic areas
Document 4: Reauthorizes the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
Increases small business award levels for participation in these programs
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Act to increase temporarily the maximum Small Business Administration participation in a loan
Raises the maximum gross loan amount for small businesses
Document 8: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from including a small business in a database until verified
Extends the Veterans' Advisory Committee on Education

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation pertaining to the operations, funding, and services of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Sets forth rules for the recalculation of annuities for employees of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS)
Prohibits USPS from reducing the frequency of mail delivery to fewer than six days each week
Document 2: Eliminates the requirement for pre-funding of the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund
Allows USPS to provide any nonpostal service or product in a manner consistent with the public interest
Document 3: Limits to six the number of United States Postal Service (USPS) officers and employees in very senior executive positions who may be paid at level I of the Executive Schedule
Prohibits USPS officers and employees in very senior executive positions from receiving fringe benefits that are greater than the benefits received by other USPS supervisory and managerial personnel
Document 4: This bill requires the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue for a four-year period a semipostal to benefit certain Department of Agriculture (USDA) conservation programs
USDA must use the transferred funds exclusively for programs authorized under title XII (Conservation) of the Food Security Act of 1985
Document 5: This bill amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to make the Postmaster General or Postmaster General designee the importer of record for non-letter class mail

The bill amends the Trade Act of 2002 to direct the Department of the Treasury to require the Postmaster General to provide for the advanced electronic transmission to the U.S. Customs and Border Protection
Document 6: Directs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue a special postage stamp to provide funding for the Department of Justice (DOJ) Gang Resistance Education and Training Program
Document 7: Directs the Postmaster General to provide for the issuance of a forever stamp that honors the sacrifices of the members of the U.S. Armed Forces
Document 9: Repeals the service standards for market-dominant products implemented by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS)
Directs USPS to reinstate the service standards that were in effect on December 31, 2011
Document 10: Extends provisions requiring the U.S. Postal Service to issue a special postage stamp for first-class mail that costs not less than 15% more than the regular first-class stamp
Requires the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and the Department of Defense (DOD) to annually report to Congress

topic.name: Voting Rights and Election Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing voter access, ensuring fair election practices, and regulating redistricting processes.

1: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
Requires such redistricting to be conducted through a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state
2: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require states to permit individuals to register to vote in a federal election on the date of the election itself
3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit the conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use
4: Makes the registration requirements of the Sex Offender Registration and Notification Act applicable to sex offenders convicted before, on, or after the enactment of that Act
5: Prohibits states and local governments from eliminating the only polling place or voter registration site on an Indian reservation
7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to make it unlawful for a chief state election administration official to take active part in political management or in a political campaign
8: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to prohibit a state or local election official from requiring an individual to present a photo identification as a condition for receiving or casting a ballot
9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals in connection with a federal, state, or local election
10: Requires states to hold special elections when a vacancy occurs in the office of U.S. Senator for the state

topic.name: Abortion and Reproductive Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures related to abortion, reproductive health, and associated privacy protections.

Document 1: require abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound
prohibits construing this Act to require a woman to view the images
Document 4: establish within the Department of Health and Human Services an Office of Pregnancy Support Services
providing services that will alleviate the financial, social, emotional, and other difficulties that may otherwise compel them to seek an abortion
Document 5: prohibits any federal funds from being provided to a hospital under title XVIII unless the hospital meets certain conditions related to a woman who is a victim of sexual assault
provides the woman with accurate and unbiased information about emergency contraception
Document 8: prohibit the federal government and any state or local government from subjecting any health professional to discrimination on the basis that the entity refuses to participate in abortion-related activities
creates a cause of action for any violation of the abortion discrimination provisions
Document 9: prohibit the Department of Health and Human Service from providing federal family planning assistance to an entity unless the entity certifies that it will not perform an abortion
excludes an abortion where the pregnancy is the result of rape or incest

topic.name: Legislation on Land and Education
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to land management, tribal affairs, and educational support initiatives.

Document 1: Cedes the District of Columbia to Maryland after Maryland's acceptance of such retrocession.
Maintains the exclusive legislative authority and control of Congress over the Area.
Document 2: Directs the Department of the Interior to take specified lands in Santa Barbara County, California, into trust for the Santa Ynez Band of Chumash Mission Indians.
The transferred land shall become part of the Santa Ynez Indian Reservation.
Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to increase the amount of loan forgiveness available under the Federal Family Education Loan programs.
Requires those low income schools to be schools at which more than 30% of the students are impoverished.
Document 4: Amends the Indian Law Enforcement Reform Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to submit to Congress a long-term plan to address incarceration in Indian country.
Establishes in the Department of Justice's criminal division an Office of Indian Country Crime.
Document 5: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Department of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities.
The federal share of each project is limited to 25%.
Document 7: Establishes the Daniel Webster Congressional Clerkship Program for the appointment of individuals who are graduates of accredited law schools.
Requires the Senate Committee on Rules and Administration and the House Committee on House Administration to each select at least six individuals for a one-year term.

topic.name: Federal Environmental and Resource Management
topic.description: This cluster addresses various federal acts related to environmental management, resource conservation, and support for specific populations affected by environmental policies.

Document 1: Amends the Departments of Veterans Affairs and Housing and Urban Development...
Limits the payment standard used in connection with a voucher for tenant-based rental assistance.
Document 2: Directs the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere to conduct a comprehensive review...
Establish a hydrocarbon monitoring and assessment program based on the recommendations.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Defense to enter into an agreement with the Office of Personnel Management...
Provide Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) coverage to eligible beneficiaries.
Document 4: Requires the Administrator of USAID to offer to enter into a contract with a nongovernmental wildlife conservation organization...
Manage an international surveillance program under which federal and non-federal partners monitor and test for the presence or arrival of avian pathogens.
Document 5: Directs the Department of the Interior to convey to Elko County...
Declares that the United States holds approximately 373 acres of BLM administered land in trust for the Te-moak Tribe.
Document 6: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) must establish a Climate Change Education Program...
Broaden the understanding of human-induced climate change, possible consequences, and potential solutions.
Document 7: Deems the Secretary of the Interior to have approved the Draft Proposed Outer Continental Shelf Oil and Gas Leasing Program...
Conduct a lease sale in each outer Continental Shelf (OCS) planning area for which there is a commercial interest.
Document 8: Amends the Clean Air Act to direct the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to...
Establish a market-based emissions cap and global warming pollutants trading program.
Document 9: Authorizes FY2015 appropriations for the Coast Guard for operation and maintenance...
Research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies related to marine environmental protection.
Document 10: Directs the President to establish a program to develop a coordinated and comprehensive federal ocean and coastal mapping program...
Enhances ecosystem approaches in decision-making for conservation and management of marine resources.

topic.name: Legal and Social Services Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving legal and social services, particularly for vulnerable populations such as victims of domestic violence, seniors, and individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: National Domestic Violence Volunteer Attorney Network Act - Authorizes the Attorney General to award grants to the American Bar Association Commission on Domestic Violence
study and report to Congress on the scope and quality of legal representation and advocacy for victims of domestic violence
Document 3: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act to authorize waivers by the Commissioner of Social Security of the five-month waiting period for entitlement to benefits based on disability
requires the Commissioner to study and report to specified congressional committees on possible improvements in disability claims processing.
Document 4: Cyber Privacy Act - Requires any Internet website that makes available to the public personal information of individuals to provide a means for individuals whose personal information it contains to request the removal of such information
Treats a violation of this Act as an unfair and deceptive act or practice.
Document 6: Securing America's Veterans Insurance Needs and Goals Act of 2010 or SAVINGS Act of 2010 - Requires a life insurance company, in order to provide life insurance for veterans
requires the insured to be given the option of specifying the manner of disbursement of insurance proceeds.
Document 8: Senior Safe Act of 2016 - Provides immunity from liability of a supervisor, compliance officer for disclosing such exploitation to a covered agency
A covered financial institution may provide such training to each of its supervisors, compliance officers.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing government accountability, transparency, and oversight in various sectors including immigration, telecommunications, labor rights, and veterans' affairs.

Document 1: amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act
prohibit or restrict a federal, state, or local government entity from undertaking any law enforcement activities
Document 2: amends the Communications Act of 1934
expand the prohibition against knowingly transmitting inaccurate caller identification information
Document 3: requires specified employers to provide each eligible employee at least 10 days of paid vacation time
conduct a public awareness campaign
Document 4: amends the Federal Advisory Committee Act
requires appointments to advisory committees to be made without regard to political affiliation
Document 5: waives a state's sovereign immunity with respect to the enforcement of uniformed services members' employment rights
increased liquidated damages against state or private employer violations
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Defense to ensure the videotaping of each strategic intelligence interrogation
develop and adopt videotaping or recording guidelines
Document 7: authorizes a covered entity to submit information on electronic health record-related adverse events
limits electronic discovery in any health care lawsuit
Document 8: amends the Ethics in Government Act of 1978
require individuals to provide a specific accounting of any financial interest in a tax haven
Document 9: amends the National Labor Relations Act to repeal the authority of the General Counsel of the NLRB
allows an aggrieved party to bring a civil action for relief
Document 10: the increasing rate of suicide among veterans is a serious problem
conduct mandatory mental health screenings for each member of the Armed Forces

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing government accountability, oversight, and public welfare through various acts related to financial management, environmental protection, and public safety.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal department, agency, or federal government contractor from making a monetary payment to any official of a foreign country for the purposes of bribery, coercion, or any illegal activity that undermines the rule of law or corrupts a public official.
Requires the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) to report to Congress on all monetary payments made by the CIA to officials of the Government of Afghanistan on or after September 11, 2001.
Document 2: Establishes in the Treasury a Reclamation Rural Water Construction Fund, into which the Secretary of the Treasury shall deposit for each of FY2014-FY2030 a specified amount of revenues that would otherwise be deposited in the reclamation fund established by the Reclamation Act of 1902.
Prohibits the Secretary from using amounts from the Fund to pay for project operation and maintenance costs.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to eligible entities so each can establish and administer American Dream Accounts for a group of at least 30 low-income public school students.

Requires American Dream Accounts to provide students with opportunities, either online or in person, to attain financial literacy; learn about preparing for enrollment in an IHE; and identify their skills or interests.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to accept the offer and simultaneously convey to Las Cienegas Conservation, LLC the Sahuarita parcel of land.

Modifies the boundary of the Las Cienegas National Conservation Area to exclude the 40-acre tract that is leased by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to the town of Elgin, Arizona, for a sanitary landfill.
Document 5: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to Israel to protect Israel's coastline and natural gas fields located in its exclusive economic zone.
Activities that may be supported by such assistance include procurement and sustainment of the David's Sling Weapon System.
Document 6: Authorize the Secretary of State to award a local guard contract abroad under the diplomatic security program on the basis of best value.
Report to Congress for each such contract providing the basis for the award and an explanation of the inability to satisfy the Department of State's needs.
Document 7: Establishes the Champlain Quadricentennial Commemoration Commission to plan, develop, and execute programs and activities to commemorate the 400th anniversary of the voyage of Samuel de Champlain.
Authorizes the Commissions to accept the services of, and provide reimbursement for, personnel detailed from New York or Vermont.
Document 8: Directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to make grants to water systems to assist in planning, designing, constructing, implementing, or maintaining any program, strategy, or infrastructure improvement.
Sets forth grant application requirements.
Document 9: Requires the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a voluntary electric and thermal energy productivity challenge grant program to provide support to no more than 25 states.
Authorizes grants to be given to public power utilities, electric cooperatives, and Indian tribes.
Document 10: Delineates passenger vessel security and safety requirements concerning vessel design, equipment, construction, and retrofitting.
Directs the Secretary to study and report to Congress on the security needs of a passenger vessel depending on the number of passengers on the vessel.

topic.name: Government Initiatives and Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing infrastructure, public safety, and resource management in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 to establish in the Department of Agriculture the Office of Rural Broadband Initiatives
administer all rural broadband-related grant and loan programs
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to establish a national Blue Alert communications network
providing assistance to states and local governments that are using Blue Alert plans
Document 4: Amends the Secure Schools and Community Self-Determination Act of 2000 to establish...an amount that is equal to 95% of the full funding amount
entitles counties or other eligible units of local government...to certain payments under the Payment in Lieu of Taxes (PILT) Program
Document 5: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish the Water Infrastructure Resiliency and Sustainability Program
provide grants to owners or operators of water systems for programs or projects to increase the resiliency or adaptability of the systems
Document 8: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to authorize the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to make grants to states
requires the Administrator to ensure that EPA's Office of Water...promote the use of green infrastructure

topic.name: Infrastructure and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to infrastructure development, maintenance, and environmental protection in the context of climate change and disaster preparedness.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Army to carry out the hurricane and storm damage reduction project, Morganza to the Gulf of Mexico, Louisiana
Makes the operation, maintenance, repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of the Houma Navigation Canal lock complex a federal responsibility.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Power Act to authorize the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to order emergency measures to protect the reliability of the bulk-power system
Directs FERC to require any owner, user, or operator of the domestic bulk-power system to implement measures to protect the system against specified vulnerabilities.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Army, acting through the Chief of Engineers, to establish a program to make grants to states to carry out harbor maintenance
Requires the chief executive officer of a state to designate a state agency to administer the financial assistance received.
Document 4: Requires the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to notify transmission organizations that they must file an electric capacity resources report
Subsequent to this report, transmission organizations must also submit tariff amendments that would achieve specified objectives.
Document 7: Directs the Administrator of NOAA and the EPA to establish an Institute for Ocean and Coastal Adaptation to Climate Change
Award grants to educational institutions for research, planning, and related activities to assess and prepare for the impacts of climate change on ocean and coastal areas.
